attempts.proven: Ark Vue menu renders role=menu tabindex=0 data-state=open NATIVELY, no React runtime; open menu is a visually CORRECT styled Kumo dropdown (rounded card, items, separator, shadow — image-verified). closed/dismissed reach exact product parity.

openStateBoundary.issue1_role: Ark exposes data-part=content AS the styled role=menu element (19 classes). Canonical Kumo exposes data-part=content as an UNSTYLED role=presentation WRAPPER with role=menu+classes on an inner div. Forcing Ark's role nesting to match canonical's wrapper structure fights the behavior library (Ark sets role=menu on its content part intrinsically; overriding creates duplicate data-part=content elements).
openStateBoundary.issue2_position: Canonical content x=5 (27px LEFT of trigger x=32); Ark default-positions under the trigger at x=32. Ark positioning prop is runtime-JS; SSR/initial-capture does not reflect it. This is Base-UI-vs-Ark popup placement divergence.
openStateBoundary.pixel: 0.7% (open is an overlay/animated state -> composite tier <=0.25% applies once placement matches; the menu is visually correct).

attempts.decisionNeeded: This is the internal-exact vs product-parity boundary the bake-off predicted (hybrid tier). The OPEN menu is a visually correct, behaviorally native Kumo dropdown. The residual failures are: (a) Ark's content part is role=menu+styled vs canonical's presentation-wrapper nesting, and (b) 27px popup x-placement. Neither is a visible defect. QUESTION FOR HUMAN: should the scorer's meaningful-part comparison for OVERLAY components compare by visible styled part (role=menu) rather than canonical's exact wrapper nesting, and should open-state placement be compared at the meaningful-part level with composite pixel tolerance? I will NOT relax the scorer unilaterally.
